Pinocchio 5.1

ValidateFits.py
Go to the documentation of this file.
1import numpy as np
2from astropy.io import fits
3import ReadPinocchio5 as rp
4import sys
5
6if len(sys.argv)<2:
7
8 print('Usage: python3 validate_fits.py [pinocchio parameter file]')
9 sys.exit(0)
10
11VERSION='v5.0'
12CATALOGS=True
13PLC=True
14HISTORIES=True
15
16params={
17 'RunFlag' :[' ','string ','name of the run','{}'],
18 'OutputList' :[' ','string ','filename with required output redshifts','{}'],
19 'BoxSize' :[' ','int ','physical size of the box [Mpc or Mpc/h]','{}'],
20 'BoxInH100' :['False','bool ','specify that the box is in Mpc/h','{}'],
21 'GridSize' :[' ','int ','number of grid points per side','{}'],
22 'RandomSeed' :[' ','int ','random seed for initial conditions','{}'],
23 'FixedIC' :['False','bool ','modulus in ICs is fixed to the average','{}'],
24 'PairedIC' :['False','bool ','phase in ICs is shifted by PI','{}'],
25 'Omega0' :[' ','float ','Omega_0 (total matter)','{}'],
26 'OmegaLambda' :[' ','float ','Omega_Lambda','{}'],
27 'OmegaBaryon' :[' ','float ','Omega_b (baryonic matter)','{}'],
28 'Hubble100' :[' ','float ','little h','{}'],
29 'Sigma8' :[' ','float ','sigma8; if 0, it is computed from P(k)','{}'],
30 'PrimordialIndex' :[' ','float ','spectral index n_s','{}'],
31 'DEw0' :[' ','float ','w0 of dark energy EoS','{}'],
32 'DEwa' :[' ','float ','wa of dark energy EoS','{}'],
33 'TabulatedEoSfile' :[' ','string ','dark energy EoS tabulated in file','{}'],
34 'FileWithInputSpectrum' :[' ','string ','P(k) tabulated in file','{}'],
35 'InputSpectrum_UnitLength_in_cm' :[' ','float ','units of tabulated P(k) [cm]','{}'],
36 'WDM_PartMass_in_kev' :[' ','float ','WDM cut [keV]','{}'],
37 'BoundaryLayerFactor' :[' ','float ','width of boundary layer for fragmentation','{}'],
38 'MaxMem' :[' ','int ','max available memory to an MPI task [Mbyte]','{}'],
39 'MaxMemPerParticle' :[' ','int ','max available memory [bytes per particle]','{}'],
40 'PredPeakFactor' :[' ','float ','guess for the number of peaks in subvolume','{}'],
41 'CatalogInAscii' :['False','bool ','catalogs are written in ascii, not binary','{}'],
42 'OutputInH100' :['False','bool ','units are in H=100 instead of true H value','{}'],
43 'NumFiles' :[' ','int ','number of files for each catalog','{}'],
44 'MinHaloMass' :[' ','int ','smallest halo in output [particles]','{}'],
45 'AnalyticMassFunction' :[' ','int ','analytic mass function given in *.mf.out','{}'],
46 'WriteTimelessSnapshot' :['False','bool ','write timeless snapshot','{}'],
47 'DoNotWriteCatalogs' :['False','bool ','skip writing catalogs (including PLC)','{}'],
48 'DoNotWriteHistories' :['False','bool ','skip writing merger histories','{}'],
49 'StartingzForPLC' :[' ','float ','starting (highest) redshift for PLC','{}'],
50 'LastzForPLC' :[' ','float ','final (lowest) redshift for PLC','{}'],
51 'PLCAperture' :[' ','float ','cone aperture for PLC [deg]','{}'],
52 'PLCProvideConeData' :['False','bool ','read vertex and direction of cone','{}'],
53 'PLCCenter' :[' ','3 floats','cone vertex [same coordinates as BoxSize]','{}'],
54 'PLCAxis' :[' ','3 floats','un-normalized direction of cone axis','{}'],
55 'CTtableFile' :[' ','string ','filename with collapse time table to read in','{}'],
56 'CAMBMatterFileTag' :[' ','string ','label for CAMB matter power spectrum files','{}'],
57 'CAMBTransferFileTag' :[' ','string ','label for CAMB transfer function files','{}'],
58 'CAMBRunName' :[' ','string ','name of CAMB run','{}'],
59 'CAMBRedshiftsFile' :[' ','string ','list of redshifts of CAMB power spectra','{}']}
60
61
62def read_param_file(param_fname):
63
64
65 try:
66 f=open(param_fname)
67 except:
68 print(f'Error: parameter file {param_fname} not found')
69 return 1
70
71 #print(f'Parameters in file: {param_fname}')
72
73 for line in f:
74
75 if line[0]=='#' or line[0]=='%':
76 continue
77
78 keys=line.split()
79
80 if len(keys)==0 or keys[0]=='%':
81 continue
82
83 if keys[0] not in params:
84 print(f"WARNING: unrecognized parameter, {keys[0]}")
85
86 else:
87
88 if params[keys[0]][0]=='False':
89 params[keys[0]][0]='True'
90 elif keys[0]=='PLCCenter' or keys[0]=='PLCAxis':
91 params[keys[0]][0]=keys[1]+' '+keys[2]+' '+keys[3]
92 else:
93 params[keys[0]][0]=keys[1]
94
95 f.close()
96
97 return 0
98
99if len(sys.argv)<2:
100
101 print('Usage: python3 validate_fits.py [pinocchio parameter file]')
102 sys.exit(0)
103
104param_fname=sys.argv[1]
105
106if read_param_file(param_fname):
107 sys.exit(0)
108
109runflag=params['RunFlag'][0]
110print(f'RunFlag: {runflag}')
111
112output_fname=params['OutputList'][0]
113
114print(f'Output list in file: {output_fname}')
115
116try:
117 outputs=np.loadtxt(output_fname,unpack=True)
118except:
119 print(f'Error: outputs file {output_fname} not found')
120 sys.exit(0)
121
122print(f'Output list: {outputs}')
123
124Nerrors=0
125
126if CATALOGS:
127 for z in outputs:
128
129 pin_fname=f'pinocchio.{z:6.4f}.{runflag}.catalog.out'
130 print(f'Reading catalog {pin_fname}')
131
132 cat1=rp.catalog(pin_fname,silent=True)
133
134 print(f'The pinocchio catalog contains the following fields: {cat1.data.dtype.names}')
135
136 fits_fname=pin_fname[:-3]+'fits'
137
138 f=fits.open(fits_fname)
139
140 names=[]
141 for i in range(1,10):
142 n=f'TTYPE{i}'
143 if n in f[1].header:
144 names.append(f[1].header[n])
145 else:
146 break
147 print(f'The fits catalog contains the following fields: {names}')
148
149 print('number of halos:',cat1.Nhalos,int(f[1].header['NHALOS']))
150 if cat1.Nhalos != int(f[1].header['NHALOS']):
151 Nerrors+=1
152 print ("ERROR: number of halos does not match!")
153
154 for field in names:
155 if ~(cat1.data[field]==f[1].data[field]).sum() >0:
156 Nerrors+=1
157 print(f'error in {field}')
158
159if PLC:
160 pin_fname=f'pinocchio.{runflag}.plc.out'
161 print(f'Reading catalog {pin_fname}')
162
163 cat1=rp.plc(pin_fname)
164
165 print(f'The catalog contains the following fields: {cat1.data.dtype.names}')
166
167 fits_fname=pin_fname[:-3]+'fits'
168
169 f=fits.open(fits_fname)
170
171 names=[]
172 for i in range(1,10):
173 n=f'TTYPE{i}'
174 if n in f[1].header:
175 names.append(f[1].header[n])
176 else:
177 break
178 print(f'The fits catalog contains the following fields: {names}')
179
180 print('number of halos:',cat1.Nhalos,int(f[1].header['NHALOS']))
181 if cat1.Nhalos != int(f[1].header['NHALOS']):
182 Nerrors+=1
183 print ("ERROR: number of halos does not match!")
184
185 for field in names:
186 if ~(cat1.data[field]==f[1].data[field]).sum() >0:
187 Nerrors+=1
188 print(f'error in {field}')
189
190
191if HISTORIES:
192 pin_fname=f'pinocchio.{runflag}.histories.out'
193 print(f'Reading catalog {pin_fname}')
194
195 cat1=rp.histories(pin_fname)
196
197 print(f'The catalog contains the following fields: {cat1.data.dtype.names}')
198
199 fits_fname=pin_fname[:-3]+'fits'
200
201 f=fits.open(fits_fname)
202
203 names=[]
204 for i in range(1,10):
205 n=f'TTYPE{i}'
206 if n in f[1].header:
207 names.append(f[1].header[n])
208 else:
209 break
210 print(f'The fits catalog contains the following fields: {names}')
211
212 print('number of trees:',cat1.Ntrees,int(f[1].header['NTREES']))
213 if cat1.Ntrees != int(f[1].header['NTREES']):
214 Nerrors+=1
215 print ("ERROR: number of trees does not match!")
216
217 for field in names:
218 if ~(cat1.data[field]==f[1].data[field]).sum() >0:
219 Nerrors+=1
220 print(f'error in {field}')
221
222 if ~(cat1.Nbranches==f[2].data['Nbranches']).sum() >0:
223 Nerrors+=1
224 print(f'error in Nbranches')
225
226 if ~(cat1.pointers==f[2].data['pointers']).sum() >0:
227 Nerrors+=1
228 print(f'error in pointers')
229
230
231
232if Nerrors==0:
233 print('*********')
234 print('no errors')
235 print('*********')
236
237
ValidateFits.read_param_file
read_param_file(param_fname)
Definition ValidateFits.py:62
